message:
fix(server): use relative deadline for MCP request timeout

BUG: Budget::with_deadline_secs(30) created an absolute deadline of
30 seconds since epoch, but wall_now() returns time since process start.
Since wall_now() > 30s, the deadline was ALWAYS exceeded immediately,
causing ALL MCP requests to timeout with 'Request timeout exceeded'.

FIX: Use wall_now() + Duration::from_secs(timeout) to create a proper
relative deadline.

Added regression tests:
- budget_deadline_is_relative_to_wall_now_not_absolute
- budget_with_deadline_secs_is_absolute_not_relative

This fix restores MCP tool functionality which was completely broken.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
